learning_outcomes.unit_name: Unit No. 1: Computer Systems and Organisation
learning_outcomes.topic: Fundamental understanding of computer architecture, software types, operating systems, logic circuits, number systems, and encoding schemes.
outcomes: Develop basic computational thinking
Explain and use data types
Appreciate the notion of algorithms
Develop a basic understanding of computer systems- architecture and operating system
Understand hardware components including CPU, memory (primary, cache, secondary), input/output devices
Identify units of memory: bit, byte, KB, MB, GB, TB, PB
Distinguish between types of software: System software, programming tools/language translators, application software
Explain functions of the operating system and OS user interface
Apply Boolean logic (NOT, AND, OR, NAND, NOR, XOR) including truth tables and De Morgan's laws
Design Logic circuits based on Boolean expressions
Perform conversions between Binary, Octal, Decimal, and Hexadecimal number systems
Understand encoding schemes: ASCII, ISCII, Unicode (UTF8, UTF32)

learning_outcomes.unit_name: Unit No. 2: Computational Thinking and Programming - I
learning_outcomes.topic: Problem-solving skills, algorithmic representation, and proficiency in Python programming including data structures, operators, control flow, and string/list/tuple/dictionary operations.
outcomes: Analyze problems to develop algorithms using decomposition
Represent algorithms using flowcharts and pseudocode
Execute simple programs (Hello World) in interactive and script modes
Identify Python tokens: keywords, identifiers, literals, operators, punctuators
Apply concepts of l-value and r-value for variables
Utilize comments effectively in code
Differentiate between mutable and immutable data types
Perform type conversion (explicit and implicit) and evaluate expressions with operator precedence
Handle input from console and display output correctly
Identify syntax, logical, and run-time errors
Implement sequential, conditional, and iterative flow of control using indentation
Write programs using if, if-else, if-elif-else statements for logic like absolute value or sorting numbers
Construct loops (for loop with range(), while loop) including nested loops, break, and continue statements

Perform string operations: concatenation, repetition, membership, slicing, traversal, and built-in methods (len, capitalize, title, lower, upper, count, find, index, endswith, startswith, isalnum, etc.)
Manipulate lists including indexing, operations (concatenation, repetition, slicing), traversal, and built-in functions/methods (append, extend, insert, remove, pop, reverse, sort)
Execute linear search on lists to find max/min/mean or count frequencies
Work with tuples: indexing, operations, nested tuples, tuple assignment, and similar analysis as lists
Manage dictionaries including accessing items by keys, mutability (adding/modifying), traversal, and built-in functions/methods (keys, values, items, get, update, del, clear)
Import and use Python modules: math (pi, e, sqrt, etc.), random, statistics

learning_outcomes.unit_name: Unit No. 3: Society, Law and Ethics
learning_outcomes.topic: Understanding of digital society, legal frameworks (IPR, IT Act), cyber safety, ethics regarding gender/disability, and environmental considerations.
outcomes: Explain the concept of Digital Footprints
Adhere to net etiquettes in communication and social media contexts
Understand Data Protection principles including Intellectual Property Rights (copyright, patent, trademark)
Identify violations of IPR: plagiarism, copyright infringement, trademark infringement
Differentiate between open source software licenses (Creative Commons, GPL, Apache)
Define Cyber Crime and identify types such as hacking, eavesdropping, phishing, fraud emails, ransomware, cyber trolls, and cyber bullying
Apply Cyber safety practices: safe browsing, identity protection, confidentiality
Identify Malware types including viruses, trojans, adware
Understand E-waste management and proper disposal of electronic gadgets
Explain the relevance of the Information Technology Act (IT Act)
Analyze technology's impact on society with specific consideration for gender and disability issues
Apply the concept of function types: built-in, module-defined, user-defined
Define arguments and parameters including default and positional parameters
Implement functions returning value(s) and understand flow of execution
Distinguish between global scope and local scope of variables
Handle exceptions using try-except-finally blocks
Manage file operations for Text files: open modes (r, r+, w, w+, a, a+), with clause, write/read methods, seek/tell manipulation
Perform basic operations on Binary files including pickle module usage (dump/load) and search/update operations
Create and manipulate CSV files using the csv module (writerow, reader)
Implement Stack data structure: push & pop operations using lists
Trace the evolution of networking: ARPANET, NSFNET, INTERNET
Identify components of data communication (sender, receiver, message, media, protocols)
Measure capacity using bandwidth and data transfer rate concepts
Understand IP addressing and switching techniques (Circuit, Packet)
Differentiate transmission media: Wired (Twisted pair, Co-axial, Fiber-optic) and Wireless (Radio waves, Micro waves, Infrared)
Identify network devices: Modem, Ethernet card, RJ45, Repeater, Hub, Switch, Router, Gateway, WIFI card
Classify networks by type (PAN, LAN, MAN, WAN) and topology (Bus, Star, Tree)
Explain network protocols: HTTP, FTP, PPP, SMTP, TCP/IP, POP3, HTTPS, TELNET, VoIP
Understand web services components: WWW, HTML, XML, domain names, URL, website, browser, server, hosting
Explain the need for databases and core concepts: relation, attribute, tuple, domain
Define degree and cardinality in relational models
Identify keys: candidate key, primary key, alternate key, foreign key
Execute Data Definition Language (DDL) commands: create/use/drop database/table/show tables/describe/alter table
Apply constraints: not null, unique, primary key
Perform Data Manipulation Language (DML) operations: insert, delete, select with operators and clauses (aliasing, distinct, where, in, between, order by, is null)
Use aggregate functions: max, min, avg, sum, count grouped by/having clause
Implement joins: cartesian product, equi-join, natural join
Connect Python with SQL databases using cursor operations (connect, execute, commit, fetchone, fetchall)
